fearGreedIndex.label: Extreme Fear
fearGreedIndex.note: All 7 CNN sub-indicators in Fear/Extreme Fear. 6th consecutive week. Bitcoin at $74K.

newPosts.title: The $7tn Oil & Gas Primer
newPosts.summary: The most comprehensive deep dive into the $7tn global oil & gas industry, released at the exact moment the Middle East conflict has rewired global supply chains. Covers upstream, midstream, and downstream dynamics, sovereignty considerations, and the actuarial blockade thesis.
newPosts.keyTakeaway: Energy sovereignty is the defining investment theme of 2026. The $7tn oil & gas sector is being repriced in real time.

newPosts.title: KITS Eyecare (KITS.TO) FINS Review
newPosts.summary: Q4 & 2025 annual filings review, downgraded from 3.5 to 3 stars. Wolf originally picked KITS at $2.58 in Jan 2023 and exited for a 500% gain. Downgrade driven by Tesla-like valuation multiples and a post-earnings sell-off of 15%+ in two days.
newPosts.keyTakeaway: KITS is a high-quality business with a stretched valuation. Post-earnings sell-off may create a better entry point.

newPosts.title: AGI Is Closer Than You Think
newPosts.summary: Dario Amodei says human-level AI may arrive sooner than expected. Prompt engineering is dead; context engineering is the new skill. AI will eat application software but proprietary data and deep workflow knowledge remain durable moats. Anthropic data: 14% drop in entry-level hiring since ChatGPT launch.
newPosts.keyTakeaway: The AGI timeline is compressing. Context engineering replaces prompt engineering. SaaS incumbents with proprietary data will survive.

newPosts.title: TMTB Weekly
newPosts.summary: Software outperformed semis by 15% in a single week — a 6-sigma event, nearly double the previous record of 8%. The prior week had flagged topping signs in leading semi charts and bottoming software charts, with the MS TMT Conference week historically amplifying such dislocations.
newPosts.keyTakeaway: The software/semi rotation is the most significant sector-level signal in years.

newPosts.title: TMTB Morning Wrap (March 18)
newPosts.summary: Today's morning wrap covers continued Iran/oil developments as the dominant macro backdrop, with Altman and Broadcom (AVGO) as key tech-specific catalysts. References Citrini's optical piece as a key read for the day.
newPosts.keyTakeaway: Oil/Iran remains the primary macro driver for tech positioning. AVGO and optical names are the key tech-specific focus.

newPosts.title: The Dead Zone
newPosts.summary: Tim Price draws a parallel between Cronenberg's 1983 film The Dead Zone and the current geopolitical moment. A literary meditation on political risk, the limits of institutional restraint, and the dangers of concentrated executive power in an era of escalating conflict.
newPosts.keyTakeaway: Political risk is underpriced. The current environment mirrors conditions that historically precede catastrophic policy errors.

mar17_update.summary: Market enters the week in Extreme Fear (20.0). S&P 500 futures liquidity collapsed to $5.1M. Crack The Market highlights mispricing in refined energy products (jet fuel, naphtha) vs crude. Boredom Baron launches 'Supply Chain Alpha' series on European small caps. Multibagger Ideas concludes ten-bagger study: FCF yield is the #1 predictor. Nate's Substack warns of AI agent data deletion risks.
